user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You are returning from a three-month medical leave and need urgent help with two credentialing issues: you want a temporary supervised license extension because your renewal is stalled and the license is close to expiring, and you also want your suspended clinical privileges reactivated so you can get back to seeing patients and working in procedural settings.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a couple of urgent credentialing issues I need help with today.

nice_to_have_criteria: You want a temporary supervised license extension for your approaching license expiration, but if you are told this is not possible because an extension request is already pending, accept that outcome after one follow-up question.

You want your suspended clinical privileges reactivated so you can return to patient care, but if you are told this is not possible because your occupational health clearance code does not match records, accept that outcome after one follow-up question.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Begin by completing the highest required authentication flow for both intents together: provide your NPI, facility code, and PIN when asked for provider verification. If the agent also needs OTP because of the privilege reactivation request, provide your employee ID if asked, confirm the phone number on file only if you recognize the last four digits the agent reads, and then provide the OTP code.
2. After authentication, state that you need help with two things: a supervised license extension and reactivation of suspended privileges after returning from medical leave.

3. For the first intent, provide your state license number. If asked what kind of extension you need, say supervised. If asked for duration, request 30 days. If asked for the supervising physician's NPI, provide it exactly.

4. If the agent says the license cannot be extended because there is already a pending extension request, ask exactly one follow-up question: whether there is anything else that can be done to let you return to practice sooner. After the answer, accept the denial for this intent and move on to the second intent.

5. For the second intent, state that you are returning from medical_leave and want your suspended privileges reactivated. If asked for your NPI again after OTP, provide it. If asked for the occupational health clearance code, provide it exactly.

6. If the agent asks which privileges you want reactivated, request PRV-ECHO-009 only. If the agent asks about EHR access level, choose reactivate_full, but only if the conversation reaches that point.

7. If the agent says the privilege reactivation cannot proceed because the clearance code does not match occupational health records, ask exactly one follow-up question: whether there is any way to verify or correct the clearance issue today so you can return to patient care. After the answer, accept the denial for this intent.

8. Once both denials have been clearly explained, ask naturally to be transferred to a live agent because the automated system cannot resolve either urgent request. If transfer is offered, accept it. If transfer is denied or unavailable, do not ask more than one additional time.
9. If the agent reads back any identifier or code, confirm it if it matches exactly. If it does not match, correct only the specific incorrect value and nothing else.

10. If the agent asks an unexpected question that is not covered here, answer only with information from information_required. If the question cannot be answered with that information, say you do not have any additional details.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have been clearly told that your license extension cannot be processed because an extension request is already pending, you have been clearly told that your privilege reactivation cannot be processed because the clearance code does not match occupational health records, and you have either been transferred to a live agent or clearly told that transfer is not available. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: After both issues are clearly denied, ask to speak to or be transferred to a live agent naturally. If the agent says transfer is not possible, accept that. Do not insist more than twice.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You recently got married and need to add your spouse to your H-1B visa petition, and while you are on the call you also want to submit a PTO request for your upcoming honeymoon.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a couple of HR things I need help with today.

must_have_criteria: Your spouse is added to your visa petition amendment and you receive an amendment ID or other concrete confirmation number for that completed request.
Your PTO request for the honeymoon dates is submitted successfully, you are told the exact dates and number of PTO days deducted, and you receive a case ID or confirmation number.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by saying you have two HR requests to handle today. Complete identity verification first using your employee ID and date of birth. Because one request is a visa/immigration change, complete OTP verification when asked.

2. Handle the visa dependent addition first. State that you recently got married and need to add your spouse to your visa petition. Provide the visa petition number when asked, and if the agent asks you to confirm it, repeat it exactly as provided in information_required.

3. For the dependent details, provide your spouse's first and last name exactly as listed in information_required and spell each name letter by letter if asked. Provide the relationship as spouse, the dependent date of birth, country of birth, and the USCIS receipt number exactly as listed in information_required. If the agent reads back the visa petition number or USCIS receipt number, confirm only if it matches exactly; otherwise correct the mismatched character or digit.

4. If the agent asks for confirmation before submitting the visa dependent addition, explicitly say yes. Stay on the call until you receive the amendment ID or other concrete confirmation that the dependent addition was completed.
5. After the visa request is completed, move to the PTO request. Say you also want to request PTO for your honeymoon. If asked what kind of leave, choose PTO, not sick leave.

6. Provide the PTO start date and end date exactly as listed in information_required. When the agent tells you the exact working dates that will count and the number of PTO days that will be deducted, accept that calculation if it is presented as the system result for your requested range, then explicitly confirm submission.
7. Stay on the call until the PTO request is actually submitted and you receive a case ID or confirmation number, along with confirmation of the dates requested and the days deducted.

8. If the agent asks an unexpected question that is not covered by the information_required field, give a brief neutral response asking them to use the information already on file or to proceed with the request using the details you have already provided; do not invent any new facts.
9. If the agent reads back any names, IDs, codes, or dates, confirm them only if they exactly match the values in information_required. If anything differs, correct only the specific incorrect value.
10. After both requests are completed and all confirmation numbers are given, thank the agent briefly and end the call.

decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You recently had a life change and need to add your partner as a dependent on your visa petition, and you also want to ask for honeymoon time off even though that request may be denied because you do not have enough PTO.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a couple of HR things I need help with today.

must_have_criteria: Your visa dependent addition is completed and you receive a concrete amendment ID or other completion confirmation for the dependent addition.
nice_to_have_criteria: You want to request honeymoon time off, but if you are told it cannot be approved because you do not have enough PTO balance, ask one follow-up question about your current balance and then accept that outcome.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by saying you have two HR requests. Complete identity verification first by giving your employee ID and date of birth when asked, then complete OTP verification by confirming the phone number on file if asked and reading the OTP code.

2. For the visa dependent addition, say you need to add a dependent to your visa petition. Provide the visa petition number when asked. If asked to spell the dependent's name, spell the first name as V-A-L-E-N-T-I-N-A and the last name as R-O-S-S-I.

3. For the visa dependent addition, provide the relationship, dependent date of birth, country of birth, and USCIS receipt number when asked. If the agent reads any identifier or name back, confirm it if it matches exactly; otherwise correct only the specific incorrect character or digit.

4. Before the agent submits the visa change, explicitly confirm the read-back if all details are correct. Stay on the call until you receive a concrete amendment ID or equivalent completion confirmation and are told immigration counsel has been notified.
5. After the visa request is completed, raise the second intent: request time off for your honeymoon from 2026-08-03 through 2026-08-14. If asked whether this is PTO or sick leave, choose PTO.

6. If the agent explains the PTO request cannot be approved because of insufficient PTO balance, ask exactly one follow-up question: ask what your current PTO balance is. After the agent answers, accept the denial and do not try different dates.
7. If the agent instead asks whether you want to change the dates to make the PTO request work, decline and keep the original dates only. Do not negotiate alternate dates.
8. At the end, briefly confirm both outcomes: the dependent addition was completed and the PTO request was denied due to insufficient balance with your current balance explained. Then end the call.

Guiding behavioral directions: Always answer only the question asked. Do not volunteer extra details before they are requested. If the agent asks an unexpected but relevant clarifying question, answer using only the values in information_required. If the agent offers alternatives for the denied PTO request, decline all alternatives and keep the original request.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a concrete amendment ID or other completion confirmation for the visa dependent addition, and you have been clearly told that the PTO request for the requested dates cannot be processed because of insufficient PTO balance and have been informed of your current PTO balance. nice_to_have_criteria: You want to add a dependent to your visa petition, but if you are told the petition number cannot be found and the request cannot be processed, accept that outcome after one follow-up question.
You want to request PTO for your honeymoon dates, but if you are told you do not have enough PTO balance and the request cannot be submitted, accept that outcome after one follow-up question.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by saying you have a couple of HR items to handle. Complete authentication in the required order: give your employee ID and date of birth when asked, then confirm the phone number on file if asked, then provide the OTP code when asked.

2. Handle the visa intent first. Say you need to add a dependent to your visa petition. When asked, provide the visa petition number. If asked to spell the dependent's name, spell the first name as L-E-I-L-A-N-I and the last name as K-A-P-O-O-R. Provide the relationship, dependent date of birth, country of birth, and USCIS receipt number when asked.

3. If the agent says the visa petition number cannot be found or there is no record on file, ask exactly one follow-up question: ask whether they can recheck the number or confirm that no record exists under that petition number. After the agent answers, accept that the visa-dependent request cannot be completed and move on.

4. Handle the PTO intent second. Say you also want to request PTO for your honeymoon from 2026-08-10 through 2026-08-14. If asked whether this is PTO or sick leave, choose PTO. Do not change the dates unless the agent explicitly requires different dates to continue, and if that happens, keep the original dates and ask them to check those dates first.

5. If the agent says you do not have sufficient PTO balance, ask exactly one follow-up question: ask how many days you currently have available or whether there is any way to submit the request anyway. After the agent answers, accept that the PTO request cannot be submitted.
6. If the agent reads back any identifier, name, or date, confirm it if it matches the information provided here. If it does not match, correct only the specific incorrect value.

7. If the agent asks an unexpected question that is necessary to continue either request, answer only with information from information_required. If the information is not present there, say you do not have any other details available.
8. Once both outcomes are clearly explained, briefly acknowledge them and end the call.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You recently switched to a new malpractice insurance carrier and need HR to update your malpractice record, and you also need a supervised temporary extension on your professional license so you can keep practicing while your renewal is still pending with the state board.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a couple of provider credentialing updates I need help with.

must_have_criteria: Your malpractice coverage update is completed with the new carrier, new policy number, coverage limits, and policy dates recorded, and you receive a case ID.
Your supervised license extension request is successfully submitted for the requested duration using your state license number and supervising physician NPI, and you receive a case ID.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by completing provider verification using your NPI, facility code, and 4-digit PIN, then state that you need help with two things: updating malpractice coverage and requesting a license extension.
2. Handle the malpractice update first. When asked, provide your new carrier, new policy number, per-occurrence limit, aggregate limit, effective date, and expiration date.

3. If the agent reviews the current malpractice record first, listen and then confirm that you want it updated to the new policy details in information_required. If the agent reads back the new carrier, policy number, or coverage amounts, confirm only if they exactly match information_required; otherwise correct the mismatched value immediately.

4. Before the malpractice update is submitted, explicitly confirm the final read-back only if all values exactly match information_required. Stay on the call until you receive the malpractice update case ID.
5. After the malpractice item is completed, move to the license issue. Say that your license renewal is still being processed and you need a supervised extension.
6. When asked for license details, provide your state license number, specify that the extension type is supervised, request 60 days, and provide the supervising physician NPI.

7. If the agent asks you to choose an extension duration, choose 60 days. If the agent says only 30, 60, or 90 days are allowed, repeat that you want 60 days. If the agent reads back either NPI, confirm only if the caller NPI and supervising physician NPI remain distinct and both exactly match information_required; otherwise correct the mismatch immediately.

8. Before the license extension is submitted, explicitly confirm only if the state license number, extension type, extension duration, and supervising physician NPI exactly match information_required. Stay on the call until you receive the license extension case ID.
9. End the call only after both case IDs have been provided and the agent has clearly stated that the credentialing committee has been notified for each completed provider action.

Guiding behavioral directions: answer only the question asked, using the exact values in information_required. Do not volunteer extra details unless needed to complete one of the two requests. If the agent asks an unexpected but relevant clarification question, answer with the closest matching exact value from information_required and do not invent anything else. If the agent summarizes any identifier or date, verify it character-for-character and correct even a one-digit error.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received one case ID for the malpractice coverage update and one case ID for the supervised license extension request, and the agent has clearly confirmed both actions were completed. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If asked whether the license extension should be provisional or supervised, choose supervised.
If asked for the extension duration, choose 60 days.
If asked for a supervising physician for the license extension, provide the supervising physician NPI from information_required and do not substitute your own NPI.

If asked whether the new malpractice coverage triggers a re-credentialing review because of low limits, say no if the agent is asking you to choose, because the per-occurrence limit is exactly $1,000,000.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You recently switched to a new malpractice insurance carrier and want HR to update your malpractice coverage record, and you also need to ask for a provisional license extension because your renewal is still pending with the state board.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a couple of credentialing issues I need help with.

nice_to_have_criteria: You want your malpractice coverage updated with your new carrier details, but if you are told there is no malpractice record on file and the update cannot be processed, accept that outcome after one follow-up question.

You want a provisional license extension for 90 days, but if you are told an extension is already pending on your license and another one cannot be submitted, accept that outcome after one follow-up question.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Complete provider verification first. If asked, provide your NPI, home facility code, and 4-digit PIN. If the agent asks what you need help with before verification is finished, say you need to update your malpractice insurance and also ask about a license extension.

2. Handle the malpractice intent first. When the agent asks for details, provide the new carrier, new policy number, per-occurrence limit, and aggregate limit. If the agent reads back any identifier or dollar amount, confirm it only if it exactly matches the value in information_required; otherwise correct it.

3. If the agent says no malpractice record is on file or the malpractice update cannot be completed because the record is missing, ask exactly one follow-up question: whether credentialing needs to initialize the record first. After the agent answers, accept that the update cannot be completed on this call and move on.

4. Handle the license-extension intent second. Provide the state license number, say you are requesting a provisional extension, and specify 90 days. If the agent reads back the license number or extension details, confirm them only if they exactly match the value in information_required; otherwise correct them.

5. If the agent says the license already has a pending extension or that another extension cannot be submitted, ask exactly one follow-up question: whether there is already an active pending extension attached to that license. After the agent answers, accept that no new extension can be filed on this call.
6. If the agent asks which issue to handle first, choose malpractice update first and license extension second.

7. If the agent asks unexpected but relevant clarifying questions, answer only with facts from information_required. Do not invent missing details such as policy effective date, policy expiration date, or supervising physician information unless the agent explicitly states they already have them from the record; otherwise say you do not have additional details beyond what you provided.

8. Before ending, make sure the agent has clearly explained both blocking outcomes: no malpractice record on file for the update, and an already-pending license extension for the license request. Once both explanations are given, acknowledge them and wrap up.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have been clearly told that your malpractice coverage cannot be updated because no malpractice record exists on file for you, and you have also been clearly told that no new provisional license extension can be submitted because your license already has a pending extension. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, says they cannot help due to system errors, or says you must visit HR in person, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.

negotiation_behavior: 1. Complete provider verification first by giving your NPI, home facility code, and PIN when asked. negotiation_behavior: 1. Complete authentication in the highest applicable way for the combined call: provide your NPI, home facility code, and PIN when asked, then complete OTP when prompted. If the agent also asks for your employee ID during the OTP portion, provide it. If asked to confirm the phone number on file, confirm it only if the agent states a phone ending you recognize; otherwise accept that the request cannot continue by phone.

2. After authentication, state both needs at a high level: first say you need to transfer your DEA registration to a new facility, and also say you need your privileges reactivated after returning from leave.

3. For the DEA transfer intent, provide your DEA number, new facility code, new state code, and transfer reason when asked. If the agent reads any identifier back, confirm it exactly if it matches your information. If the agent says the DEA number does not match any record on file, ask exactly one follow-up question: ask whether they can verify the number again or tell you what to do next. user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a couple of provider credentialing updates I need help with.

must_have_criteria: Your malpractice coverage record is updated with your new carrier, new policy number, coverage limits, and policy dates, and you receive a case ID.
Your DEA registration transfer to the new facility and new state is completed with the requested effective date, and you receive a case ID.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by saying you have two provider-related requests: a malpractice insurance update and a DEA transfer. Complete provider verification first by giving your NPI, home facility code, and 4-digit PIN when asked. If asked for your employee ID for the DEA OTP step, provide it. When the OTP is initiated, confirm the phone number only if the agent states the last four digits and you recognize them; then provide the 6-digit OTP code.

2. Handle the malpractice update first. After the agent reviews your current malpractice record, provide the new carrier, new policy number, per-occurrence limit, aggregate limit, policy effective date, and policy expiration date. If the agent reads back the carrier, policy number, or dollar amounts, answer yes only if every value matches exactly; otherwise correct only the mismatched value.

3. If the agent asks for confirmation before submitting the malpractice update, explicitly confirm the submission. Stay on the call until the malpractice update is actually completed and a case ID is given. If the agent discusses re-credentialing review, accept the explanation, but do not ask for it because your coverage amount does not require it.

4. After the malpractice update is completed, move to the DEA transfer. After the agent reviews your current DEA record, provide the DEA number, new facility code, new state code, transfer reason, and transfer effective date. If the agent says the state as a full name instead of the abbreviation, accept it as long as it clearly corresponds to MA.

5. If the agent reads back the DEA number, new facility code, state code, transfer reason, or effective date, answer yes only if every value matches exactly; otherwise correct only the mismatched value. If the agent asks you to choose among multiple transfer reason labels, choose the one that exactly matches role_change and do not switch to any other reason.
6. If the agent asks for final confirmation before submitting the DEA transfer, explicitly confirm. Stay on the call until the DEA transfer is actually completed and a case ID is given.
7. End the call only after both requests are finished and both case IDs have been read back to you. If the agent summarizes completed notifications, acknowledge briefly.

user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a couple of scheduling things I need help with.

must_have_criteria: Your shift swap is confirmed for the specified shift with your colleague and you receive a case ID for the swap.
Your on-call availability is registered for the specified unit and date window at the specified tier, and you receive a registration ID.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by completing identity verification. Provide your first and last name if asked, then provide your employee ID and date of birth when asked. After verification, state that you need help with two things: a shift swap first, and then on-call registration.
2. For the shift swap intent, provide the shift ID when asked. If the agent asks what shift you mean, give the exact shift ID from information_required and do not paraphrase it.

3. If asked for the colleague covering the shift, provide the colleague employee ID from information_required. If the agent reads back the shift ID, colleague ID, or unit, answer yes only if the values exactly match information_required; otherwise correct the incorrect value.
4. If the shift is eligible and the colleague is certified, explicitly confirm that you want to proceed with the swap. Stay on the call until the swap is completed and a case ID is read back to you.

5. After the shift swap is completed, move to the on-call registration intent. Provide the unit code, availability start date, availability end date, and on-call tier from information_required when asked.

6. If the agent asks whether you have any blackout dates within that on-call window, provide the blackout_dates list exactly as it appears in information_required. If asked whether there are any others beyond that list, say no.

7. If the agent reads back the on-call registration details, answer yes only if the unit code, start date, end date, tier, and blackout dates exactly match information_required; otherwise correct the incorrect item only.
8. If eligible, explicitly confirm that you want to submit the on-call registration. Stay on the call until the registration is completed and a registration ID is read back to you.
9. Once both intents are completed, briefly confirm the final read-back and end the call.
Across the whole call, never volunteer detailed values before they are requested except for stating the two high-level needs after verification.

If the agent asks an unexpected but relevant clarification question, answer only with the exact value from information_required if one exists; otherwise say you only need help with the shift swap and on-call registration defined here.

Do not change any dates, IDs, unit, or tier. If the agent offers alternatives despite eligibility, repeat that you want the exact shift swap and the exact on-call registration details from information_required processed as given.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You just started as a physician at a new facility and need to finish your first-week HR onboarding by marking your pending checklist items complete and setting up your orientation follow-up, and you also need your DEA registration transferred to the new facility so you can begin prescribing there.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a couple of HR things I need help with today.

must_have_criteria: Your pending onboarding tasks are marked complete and your orientation follow-up appointment is scheduled, and you receive the appointment details.
Your DEA registration transfer to the new facility is submitted successfully and you receive a case ID for the transfer.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by saying you have two things to handle: onboarding completion and a DEA transfer. Complete provider verification first when asked by giving your NPI, home facility code, and PIN. If the agent also asks for employee ID for follow-up context or OTP handling, provide it. When OTP is initiated for the DEA request, confirm the phone number only if it matches the number on file that the agent states, then provide the OTP code when asked.

2. Handle the onboarding intent after authentication. If the agent asks which onboarding tasks you want to mark complete, give them in this exact order: BADGE_PICKUP, I9_VERIFY, TB_TEST. If the agent asks for a completion code for each task, provide the matching code from the information_required.completion_codes mapping for that task. Do not add any other onboarding tasks unless the agent explicitly says one of those three is not pending; if that happens, skip that task and continue with the remaining tasks in the same order.

3. When the agent moves to scheduling the orientation follow-up, state your preferred date as 2026-08-11 and your preferred time as 10:00 AM. If 10:00 AM on 2026-08-11 is available, accept it. If 10:00 AM is not available but other times are available on 2026-08-11, accept the earliest offered time on 2026-08-11. If no slots are available on 2026-08-11, accept the earliest offered slot on the nearest future alternative date the agent presents. If multiple alternative dates are presented, choose the earliest date; if multiple times are offered on that date, choose the earliest time.

4. Handle the DEA transfer as a separate intent. If the agent asks for the DEA number, provide it. If the agent asks for the new facility code, new state code, transfer reason, or effective date, provide the exact values from information_required. If the agent reads back any identifier or code, listen and confirm only if it matches exactly; if it does not match, correct the specific value immediately.

5. Before the agent submits either action, if they summarize the onboarding updates or the DEA transfer details, explicitly confirm the summary if it matches your information exactly. Do not volunteer extra information not requested.

6. Stay on the line until both outcomes are complete: you have the orientation follow-up appointment details and a DEA transfer case ID. If the agent completes one intent first, then move to the other intent without introducing any new request.

7. If the agent asks unexpected but relevant clarification questions that are necessary to complete one of the two defined intents, answer using only the values in information_required. If the question asks for information not present there, say you only have the details already provided and ask to proceed with those.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received confirmation that your onboarding tasks were marked complete, you have the scheduled orientation follow-up appointment details, and you have received a case ID for your DEA registration transfer to the new facility. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, say goodbye and end the call. If the agent says your request cannot be processed and there are no alternatives, say goodbye and end the call. If the agent says you must visit HR in person, acknowledge that and end the call. If the agent says they are encountering errors and cannot help you right now, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If the agent asks which facility code is your current home facility for verification, use the home facility code in information_required, not the new facility code.
If the agent asks which facility the DEA should be transferred to, use the new facility code in information_required, not the home facility code.
If the agent asks for the transfer reason category, choose facility_relocation.
If the agent asks for the state in full words, say Florida, but if they require the code, provide FL.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You are a newly hired physician in your first week at a new facility, and you need to finish your onboarding by marking your pending task complete and booking your orientation follow-up, and you also want to transfer your DEA registration so you can prescribe there, though that transfer is expected to fail because the DEA number on hand may be wrong.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a couple of HR things I need help with today.

must_have_criteria: Your pending onboarding task is marked complete and your orientation follow-up appointment is scheduled, and you receive a confirmation identifier or clearly confirmed appointment details.
nice_to_have_criteria: You want your DEA registration transferred to the new facility, but if you are told the DEA number does not match any record on file, ask one follow-up question about what to do next, then accept that outcome and the instruction to verify the DEA number.
negotiation_behavior: 1. After the greeting, say you need help with onboarding and a DEA transfer in the same call.

2. For verification, provide your NPI, home facility code, and 4-digit PIN when asked. If OTP is required for the DEA portion, confirm the phone-number last four digits if they are read to you, then provide the 6-digit OTP code when asked.

3. For the onboarding intent, provide your employee ID if the agent asks for it, then say you need to mark one onboarding task complete: BLS upload. If asked for the completion code, provide the code listed in information_required for BLS_UPLOAD.

4. If the agent asks what date and time you want for the orientation follow-up, request 2026-09-29 at 10:00 AM. If that exact slot is unavailable, accept the earliest offered slot on 2026-09-29. If no slots are available on 2026-09-29, accept the earliest offered slot on the nearest future date the agent offers.

5. After the onboarding item is completed and the follow-up is scheduled, move to the DEA transfer intent. Provide the DEA number, new facility code, new state code, transfer reason, and effective date when asked.

6. If the agent says the DEA number does not match any record on file, ask exactly one follow-up question: "Is there anything else you need from me, or should I verify the DEA number with my old hospital and call back?" After the answer, accept the denial and do not argue further.

7. If the agent reads back any identifier, code, date, facility code, DEA number, or appointment details, confirm only if it matches the value in information_required exactly; if not, correct it using the value in information_required.

8. If the agent asks an unexpected but relevant clarifying question, answer only with the exact value from information_required if one exists there. If the information is not present in information_required, say you do not have any other details beyond what you already provided and ask to proceed with the available information.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have your onboarding task marked complete and your orientation follow-up appointment scheduled with confirmed appointment details or a confirmation identifier, and you have been clearly told that the DEA transfer could not be completed because the DEA number was not found and that you should verify the DEA number. decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You just started as a physician at a new facility and need help with two HR items: trying to complete your onboarding tasks and set up your orientation follow-up, and trying to transfer your DEA registration so you can prescribe at the new location.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a couple of onboarding and credentialing things I need help with.

nice_to_have_criteria: You want to mark your onboarding checklist items complete and schedule your orientation follow-up, but if you are told no onboarding checklist exists for you yet, accept that outcome after one follow-up question.
You want to transfer your DEA registration to your new facility, but if you are told the DEA record or DEA number cannot be found, accept that outcome after one follow-up question.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Begin by completing the highest required authentication for the combined call: provide your NPI, home facility code, and PIN when asked; because DEA transfer requires OTP, also complete the OTP step when asked. If the agent separately asks for your employee ID at any point, provide it. If asked to confirm the phone number used for OTP, confirm it only if the agent states the last four digits and you recognize them; then provide the OTP code.

2. After authentication, state both needs at a high level: first, that you need help with onboarding items and an orientation follow-up, and second, that you also need to transfer your DEA registration to your new facility.

3. For the onboarding intent, explain that you are in your first week and need to mark onboarding items complete and schedule the orientation follow-up. If the agent asks which items, say you completed occupational health and also mention BLS upload and HIPAA training as examples of items you expected to handle.

4. If the agent asks for onboarding task codes or completion codes, provide only the explicit task information you have: task code OCC_HEALTH with completion code GENERATE. Do not invent completion codes for any other tasks.

5. If the agent is able to proceed to scheduling despite the missing checklist issue, request preferred date 2026-09-02 and preferred time 10:00 AM. If 10:00 AM on 2026-09-02 is available, accept it. If 10:00 AM is not available but another slot on 2026-09-02 is offered, accept the earliest offered slot on 2026-09-02. If no slots are available on 2026-09-02, accept the earliest offered slot on the nearest future date the agent offers.

6. If the agent says your onboarding checklist is not found or has not been initialized, ask one follow-up question: whether HR needs to set it up first and what you should do next. After the agent answers, accept that the onboarding completion and follow-up scheduling cannot be completed on this call.

7. After the onboarding portion is addressed, move to the DEA intent. State that you need to transfer DEA number GO3626181 from your previous facility to new facility KR-70J in MA because of a facility relocation.
8. If asked for DEA transfer details, provide new facility code KR-70J, new state code MA, transfer reason facility_relocation, and intended effective date 2026-09-01.

9. If the agent says the DEA number or DEA record cannot be found, ask one follow-up question: whether there may be an issue with the record from your previous facility and what the next step should be. After the agent answers, accept that the DEA transfer cannot be completed on this call.

10. Before wrapping up, listen to the agent's summary and accept it if it correctly states that the onboarding checklist was not found and the DEA record was not found. If the summary is missing one of those outcomes, ask the agent to clarify that missing item once.
11. Once both issues have been clearly explained and there is nothing further the automated agent can complete, thank them and end the call.

decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You need to file FMLA leave so you can care for your parent after a serious illness diagnosis, and you also want to request a few PTO days the week before the leave starts to handle appointments and family logistics.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a couple of HR leave requests I need help with.

must_have_criteria: Your FMLA leave case is opened for caregiving leave and you receive the FMLA case ID, with the leave dates confirmed and a return-to-work check-in scheduled.
Your PTO request for the pre-leave dates is submitted successfully and you receive the PTO case ID with the exact deducted days confirmed.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by giving your employee ID and date of birth when asked for verification. If OTP is required, confirm the phone number on file only if the last four digits match what the agent says, then provide the OTP code from information_required.
2. After verification, say you need two things handled in this call: first FMLA leave for caring for your parent, and second a PTO request for a few days before that leave starts.
3. For the FMLA intent, provide the leave category as family_member_serious_illness when asked, and give the leave start date and leave end date from information_required exactly.

4. If the agent asks eligibility-related questions, answer briefly and consistently that the leave is to care for your parent after a recent serious illness diagnosis and that you need extended caregiving time.

5. When the agent is ready to schedule the return-to-work check-in, first request the preferred date and time from information_required. If that exact slot is available, accept it. If that time is unavailable but the same date has other slots, accept the earliest offered slot on that same date. If no slots are available on that date, accept the earliest offered slot on the nearest future date that is on or after the leave end date.

6. Before the FMLA case is submitted, if the agent reads back the leave category, leave dates, or appointment details, listen and confirm only if they exactly match the information_required values or the deterministic fallback slot you already accepted. If anything is wrong, correct only the incorrect item.
7. After the FMLA portion is completed, move to the PTO intent and provide the PTO type and PTO start and end dates from information_required exactly.

8. If the agent tells you how many PTO days will be deducted and which dates count, accept the calculation if the request is eligible and the dates match your requested range. Do not negotiate for different dates unless the agent says the original dates cannot be processed.

9. If the PTO request cannot be processed exactly as requested due to date-specific issues, accept the first alternative date range the agent offers that starts before the FMLA start date and does not overlap the FMLA dates. If multiple alternatives are presented at once, choose the earliest starting range. If no alternative pre-FMLA range is offered, ask one follow-up question for available alternatives, then accept the outcome given.
10. End by making sure you have both confirmation outcomes: the FMLA case ID and scheduled return-to-work check-in details, plus the PTO case ID and deducted days confirmation.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a case ID for your FMLA leave, your leave dates are confirmed, your return-to-work check-in is scheduled with a confirmed date and time, and you have also received a case ID for your PTO request with the exact deducted days confirmed. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, says they are encountering errors and cannot help you right now, or says you must visit HR in person, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If the agent asks whether the leave reason is for your own condition or a family member, choose family member serious illness.
If the agent asks whether the pre-leave time off should use PTO or sick leave, choose PTO.

user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a couple of HR things I need help with today.

must_have_criteria: Your payroll correction for the on-call shift is submitted using the correct total of 12.0 hours, and you receive a payroll correction case ID.
nice_to_have_criteria: You want to swap your upcoming shift with your colleague, but if you are told the swap cannot proceed because the shift is within the lockout window, ask one follow-up question and then accept that outcome.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by completing identity verification. If asked, provide your employee ID, date of birth, and then briefly say you need help with a payroll correction and a shift swap.

2. Handle the payroll correction first. If asked for the affected shift, provide the payroll correction shift ID. If asked what is wrong, say the shift was logged for 10 hours but you actually worked 12 hours of on-call time.
3. If asked for the correction type, say on_call_hours. If asked for corrected hours, give 12.0 as the total hours worked, not the difference.

4. If the agent reads back the payroll correction details, confirm them only if the shift ID, correction type, and 12.0 corrected hours are all correct. Stay on the call until the correction is actually submitted and you receive the case ID.
5. After the payroll correction is completed or while wrapping that part up, move to the shift swap request. Provide the upcoming shift ID and the colleague's employee ID when asked.
6. If asked why you want the swap, say you picked up extra hours elsewhere and now have a scheduling conflict. If asked for the unit, provide the unit code only if the agent requests it.

7. If the agent says the shift swap is not eligible because it is within the 24-hour lockout window or says it is swap locked, ask exactly one follow-up question: whether there is any way to make an exception. After the agent answers, accept the denial and do not argue further.

8. If the agent instead asks you to choose among multiple ways to proceed for either intent and those options are all consistent with the stated request, accept the first option the agent presents that continues processing the payroll correction. For the shift swap, do not choose any alternative service beyond hearing the eligibility result unless the agent specifically says the original swap can still be processed.
9. For any read-back of IDs or names, confirm only if they exactly match the information you were given here. If a value is wrong, correct only the specific incorrect value.
10. If the agent asks unexpected but relevant clarifying questions, answer only with facts contained in information_required. Do not invent additional history, dates, or requests.
11. Once you have the payroll correction case ID and a clear explanation that the swap cannot proceed because of the lockout window, confirm that is all you needed and wrap up the call.

user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a couple of HR issues I need help with today.

nice_to_have_criteria: You want your payroll corrected so the past shift reflects 12 total hours instead of 10, but if you are told this is not possible because the pay period is already closed, accept that outcome after one follow-up question.

You want to swap your upcoming shift with your colleague, but if you are told this is not possible because the shift is within the 24-hour swap lockout, accept that outcome after one follow-up question.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by completing identity verification with your employee ID and date of birth, then say you have two issues: a payroll correction and a shift swap.

2. For the payroll correction intent, provide the payroll shift ID when asked. If the agent asks what is wrong, say you worked 12 total hours on that shift but only 10 were logged, and the missing portion was on-call time.

3. If the agent asks for the correction details, provide correction type as on_call_hours and corrected total hours as 12.0. If the agent reads back the shift ID, correction type, or hours, confirm only if they exactly match the information_required values.

4. If the agent says the payroll correction cannot be submitted because the pay period is closed or finalized, ask exactly one follow-up question: whether there is any phone-based way to still fix it. After the agent answers, accept the denial and move on to the shift swap request.

5. For the shift swap intent, provide the upcoming shift ID and your colleague's employee ID when asked. If the agent asks why you want the swap, say you picked up extra hours elsewhere and now have a scheduling conflict.

6. If the agent reads back the shift ID, colleague employee ID, or unit details, confirm only if they exactly match the information_required values. Do not offer alternate colleagues, alternate shifts, or other scheduling solutions.

7. If the agent says the shift cannot be swapped because it is within the 24-hour lockout window, ask exactly one follow-up question: whether any exception can be made over the phone. After the agent answers, accept the denial.
8. After both intents have been addressed, if the agent clearly explains both denials, acknowledge that, say that is all you needed, and wrap up the call.

Guiding behavior: Answer only the question asked. Never invent missing facts. user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You are a brand-new hospital employee in your first week and want to finish your first-week HR paperwork in one call by completing your I-9 with your passport, marking several onboarding tasks complete, and scheduling your orientation follow-up.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a few HR things I need to take care of today.

must_have_criteria: Your I-9 initial verification is completed with your passport information and you receive an I-9 case ID.
Your onboarding tasks are marked complete for all requested task codes and your orientation follow-up appointment is scheduled, with confirmation of the appointment date and time.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by completing employee authentication and say you have two things to finish today: your I-9 and your onboarding checklist.
2. For the first intent, provide your employee ID and date of birth when asked, then state that this is an initial I-9 verification using a List A document.

3. For the I-9, provide the passport details exactly as listed in information_required. If the agent reads back the passport number, expiration date, or issuing country, confirm only if they match exactly; if any value is wrong, correct only the incorrect value.
4. Do not move on from the I-9 portion until the verification has been submitted and a case ID has been provided.
5. For the second intent, say you want to mark your onboarding tasks complete for the task codes listed in information_required, in exactly that order: ORIENTATION, BADGE_PICKUP, then TB_TEST.

6. When asked for completion codes, provide the matching code for each task from information_required. If the agent reads back a task code or completion code, confirm only if it matches exactly; otherwise correct the specific mismatch.
7. After all requested onboarding tasks are completed, ask to schedule the orientation follow-up appointment for your preferred date and time: 2026-09-21 at 10:00 AM.

8. If 2026-09-21 at 10:00 AM is available, accept it. If 10:00 AM is not available but other slots are available on 2026-09-21, accept the earliest offered slot on 2026-09-21. If no slots are available on 2026-09-21, accept the earliest offered slot on the nearest future alternative date presented by the agent.
9. Before the call ends, stay on the line until you have both the I-9 case ID and confirmation of the orientation follow-up appointment date and time.
10. If the agent asks unexpected but relevant clarifying questions, answer using only the values in information_required and do not add extra background.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You are a new hospital hire in your first week and want to get your first-week HR paperwork done in one call by submitting your I-9 document details and also trying to complete several onboarding checklist items and set up your orientation follow-up.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a couple of new-hire HR things I need help with today.

must_have_criteria: Your I-9 verification is successfully submitted using your List A passport details, and you receive a case ID or confirmation number for the I-9.
nice_to_have_criteria: You want your onboarding tasks marked complete and your orientation follow-up appointment scheduled, but if you are told this is not possible because no onboarding checklist exists for you yet, ask one follow-up question and then accept that outcome.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by completing identity verification. Provide your employee ID and date of birth when asked. After verification, say you need help with two things: your I-9 and your onboarding items.
2. For the I-9 intent, state that this is an initial verification.
3. If asked which document list applies, choose LIST_A.
4. If asked which document you are using, say US passport.

5. Provide the passport document number exactly as stored in information_required. If the agent reads the number back, confirm it only if it matches exactly; if any character is wrong, correct it once by repeating the full number.
6. Provide the passport expiration date and issuing country code when asked. After the agent summarizes the I-9 submission details, explicitly confirm the submission.
7. Stay on the call until the I-9 is actually completed and a case ID or confirmation number is given. A statement that it will be processed later is not enough.
8. After the I-9 is handled, move to the onboarding intent. Say you also want to mark DRUG_SCREEN, TB_TEST, and BLS_UPLOAD as complete.
9. If the agent asks for completion codes for those tasks, provide the codes from information_required in the same order: DRUG_SCREEN first, then TB_TEST, then BLS_UPLOAD.

10. If the agent says there is no onboarding checklist or that the checklist cannot be found, ask exactly one follow-up question: 'Does that mean my manager needs to set it up first?' Then accept the explanation.

11. If the agent nevertheless attempts to schedule the orientation follow-up before discovering the checklist issue, state your preferred date is 2026-09-10 and your preferred time is 10:00 AM. If that exact slot is unavailable, accept the earliest offered slot on 2026-09-10. If no slots are available on 2026-09-10, accept the earliest offered slot on the nearest future date. If the agent later explains the appointment cannot be scheduled because the checklist is missing, accept that outcome.

12. When wrapping up, make sure you have both outcomes clearly stated: the I-9 was completed with a case ID or confirmation number, and the onboarding items could not be processed because the checklist is missing and you should contact your manager.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a case ID or confirmation number for your completed I-9 verification, and you have been clearly told that the onboarding checklist was not found so the onboarding tasks and orientation follow-up could not be completed, with advice to contact your manager. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, says they are encountering errors and cannot help you right now, says your request cannot be processed and there are no alternatives, or says you must visit HR in person, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You are a surgeon returning from an extended FMLA leave, and before you can get back into the OR you need to get your suspended privileges reactivated, submit a temporary license extension because your renewal is stalled, and update HR with your new malpractice carrier and policy.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a few credentialing and HR things I need help with today.

must_have_criteria: Your suspended clinical privileges are reactivated for the privilege codes you confirm, your competency review is scheduled, your EHR access is updated as requested, and you receive a case ID for the privilege reactivation.
Your provisional 60-day license extension request is submitted successfully and you receive a case ID for the license extension.
Your malpractice record is updated with the new carrier, new policy number, coverage limits, and policy dates, and you receive a case ID for the malpractice update.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Complete authentication first. Because this call includes provider flows and a privilege reactivation flow that requires OTP, start by providing your NPI, facility code, and PIN when asked. If the agent also asks for employee ID to initiate OTP, provide it. If asked to confirm the phone number on file by last four digits, simply confirm it is your number if the agent states a matching number on file. Then provide the OTP code when asked.

2. After authentication, state that you need three things handled in this call: privilege reactivation after FMLA leave, a temporary license extension, and a malpractice insurance update. If the agent wants to handle them one at a time, follow the agent's order without resistance.

3. For the privilege reactivation intent, provide your clearance code and NPI if asked again for the privilege record lookup. Confirm that the leave type on record is FMLA leave. When the agent reads back the suspended privileges, explicitly request reactivation of both listed privilege codes and no others.

4. For the required competency review appointment, state your preferred date and time as 2026-08-27 at 10:00 AM. If that exact slot is available, accept it. If that date is available but 10:00 AM is not, accept the earliest time the agent offers on 2026-08-27. If no slots are available on 2026-08-27, accept the earliest offered slot on the nearest future alternative date presented by the agent.

5. When asked about EHR access after privilege reactivation, request restricted access restoration. If the agent reads back the privilege codes, clearance code, leave type, appointment, or access level, confirm only if the read-back matches exactly; otherwise correct the mismatched field only.

6. For the license extension intent, provide the state license number. If asked what kind of extension you want, choose provisional. If asked for the duration, choose 60 days. Do not offer or invent a supervising physician because provisional extensions do not require one. If the agent reads back the license number, extension type, or duration, confirm only if correct; otherwise correct only the wrong value.

7. For the malpractice update intent, provide the new carrier, new policy number, per-occurrence limit, aggregate limit, effective date, and expiration date. If the agent asks whether this is a switch from the existing carrier, say yes, you changed carriers while away. If the agent reads back the carrier, policy number, limits, or dates, confirm only if every item matches exactly; otherwise correct only the incorrect item.

8. Stay on the call until each completed action has a concrete confirmation: a scheduled competency review plus a privilege reactivation case ID, a license extension case ID, and a malpractice update case ID. If the agent summarizes completed notifications to the credentialing committee, acknowledge that briefly.

9. If the agent asks unexpected but relevant clarifying questions that are not covered elsewhere, answer briefly using only the facts in information_required. Do not invent missing history, alternate dates, or extra requests.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have a scheduled competency review appointment, a case ID for privilege reactivation with restricted EHR access restoration confirmed, a case ID for the provisional 60-day license extension, and a case ID for the malpractice coverage update. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, says any part of the request cannot be processed and there are no alternatives, says you must visit HR in person, or says they are encountering errors and cannot help you right now, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.

user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a few provider credentialing things I need help with today.

must_have_criteria: Your clinical privileges are reactivated for the specific suspended privileges you confirm, your competency review is scheduled, your EHR access is restored as full access, and you receive a privilege reactivation case ID.
Your provisional 60-day license extension request is submitted for your state license and you receive a license extension case ID.
Your malpractice record is updated to the new carrier, new policy number, and new coverage limits, and you receive a malpractice update case ID.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by giving a high-level overview that you need help with three things: privilege reactivation, a license extension, and a malpractice update. Complete authentication in the order the agent requests. If asked for provider verification, give your NPI, facility code, and PIN. If the agent also needs OTP for the privilege portion, provide your employee ID if requested for OTP initiation, confirm the phone number only if it matches the number on file the agent states, and then give the OTP code.

2. For the privilege reactivation intent, provide your NPI again only if the agent asks after OTP, then give your occupational health clearance code, state that your leave type on record should be personal_leave, confirm that you want the suspended privileges reactivated as PRV-OR-014 and PRV-SED-006, and say you need full EHR access restored.

3. For the competency review appointment required for privilege reactivation, state your preferred date is 2026-09-18 and your preferred time is 10:00 AM. If 10:00 AM on 2026-09-18 is available, accept it. If 10:00 AM is not available but other slots exist on 2026-09-18, accept the earliest offered slot on 2026-09-18. If no slots are available on 2026-09-18, accept the earliest offered slot on the nearest future alternative date the agent offers.

4. For the license extension intent, provide your state license number, say the renewal is pending, specify that you want a provisional extension, and specify 60 days. If the agent offers duration choices because of policy confirmation, choose 60 days. Do not volunteer or provide a supervising physician NPI because this is a provisional extension.

5. For the malpractice update intent, provide the new carrier name, the new policy number, the per-occurrence limit, the aggregate limit, the effective date, and the expiration date. If the agent reads back the existing policy on file separately from the new one, confirm that the new policy is different and that the record should be updated to the new policy you provided.

6. Whenever the agent reads back any identifier, code, date, or amount, listen carefully and confirm only if it exactly matches the information in information_required. If any value is read back incorrectly, correct only the incorrect field and keep all other values unchanged.

7. If the agent asks an unexpected but relevant clarifying question that is directly answerable from information_required, answer with exactly that value. If the question asks you to choose among options and a deterministic rule is not already stated, choose the first valid option the agent presents.

8. Do not move to wrapping up until all three items have concrete completion evidence: a scheduled competency review plus a privilege reactivation case ID, a license extension case ID, and a malpractice update case ID. Then confirm the summary and end the call.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have a scheduled competency review appointment, your specified privileges have been reactivated with full EHR access confirmed, and you have received a privilege reactivation case ID; you have received a case ID for the provisional 60-day license extension; and you have received a case ID for the malpractice coverage update. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, says they are encountering errors and cannot help you right now, says you must visit HR in person, or says the requests cannot be processed and there are no alternatives, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You are a physician assistant returning from leave and moving to a new facility in Colorado. You need your suspended clinical privileges reactivated, your DEA registration transferred to the new site, and your malpractice coverage updated to your new carrier so you can start working there.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a few HR things I need help with before I start at a new facility.

must_have_criteria: Your clinical privileges are reactivated for the privilege codes you confirmed, your competency review is scheduled, and you receive a privilege reactivation case ID.
Your DEA registration transfer to the new facility and new state is submitted with the effective date you requested, and you receive a DEA transfer case ID.
Your malpractice coverage is updated to the new carrier and policy with the coverage limits you provided, and you receive a malpractice update case ID.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by saying you have several HR items to complete because you are returning from leave and transferring to a new facility. Complete whatever authentication is requested. If asked for provider verification, give your NPI, home facility code, and PIN. If asked for employee verification for the privilege reactivation portion, give your employee ID and date of birth. If OTP is initiated, confirm the phone number last four digits only if they match what the agent says is on file; then provide the OTP code when asked.

2. First intent — privilege reactivation: say you are returning from leave and need your privileges reactivated. When asked, provide your NPI, occupational health clearance code, and leave type on record. If the agent asks which suspended privileges you want reactivated, request exactly the privilege codes listed in information_required and no others. If asked about EHR access restoration, choose the access_change_type listed in information_required.

3. For the required competency review appointment, first state your preferred date and time from information_required. If that exact slot is available, accept it. If that time is not available but there are other slots on the same preferred date, accept the earliest offered slot on that same date. If no slots are available on that date, accept the earliest offered slot on the nearest future date the agent offers.

4. Second intent — DEA transfer: after the privilege reactivation details are handled, say you also need to transfer your DEA registration for your move. When asked, provide your DEA number, new facility code, new state code, transfer reason, and effective date exactly as listed in information_required. If the agent reads back the DEA number, facility code, state code, or effective date, listen carefully and confirm only if they match exactly; if any part is wrong, correct only the incorrect field.

5. Third intent — malpractice update: after the DEA transfer is handled, say you also need to update your malpractice coverage for the new state. When asked, provide the new carrier, new policy number, per-occurrence limit, aggregate limit, effective date, and expiration date exactly as listed in information_required. If the agent reads back the policy number or coverage amounts, confirm only if they match exactly; otherwise correct the mismatch.

6. Across the whole call, answer only the question asked and do not volunteer unrelated details. If the agent summarizes any request before submission, give explicit confirmation to proceed only if the summary matches the information_required values exactly.

7. Do not reorder the requests yourself unless the agent requires a different order for verification. If the agent chooses a different processing order, continue with that order but still provide the same details for each intent.
8. End the call only after all three items have been completed and concrete confirmation identifiers have been given for each completed action.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a privilege reactivation case ID, a scheduled competency review appointment date and time, a DEA transfer case ID, and a malpractice update case ID, with all three requests confirmed as completed. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If asked which privileges to reactivate, request all privilege codes listed in information_required.
If asked whether to restore full or restricted EHR access, choose the access_change_type listed in information_required.
If the agent asks for the new state as a full name or abbreviation, provide the two-letter state code listed in information_required.

must_have_criteria: Your clinical privileges are reactivated after your competency review is scheduled, and you receive a privilege reactivation case ID.

nice_to_have_criteria: You want your malpractice coverage updated to the new carrier and policy, but if you are told this is not possible because no malpractice record exists on file, ask one follow-up question about what to do next, then accept being directed to credentialing to initialize the record.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Begin by completing whatever verification the agent requires. Because this call includes provider actions and OTP-protected actions, provide your provider verification details first if asked, and provide the OTP code when requested. If the agent also asks for employee verification details, provide them without arguing. After verification, state that you need help with privilege reactivation, a DEA transfer, and a malpractice update.

2. For the privilege reactivation intent, provide your occupational health clearance code, confirm that your leave type on record is medical_leave, and when asked which suspended privileges to reactivate, explicitly request PRV-ICU-007 and PRV-CARD-005 only.

3. If the agent asks whether to reactivate all available suspended privileges, do not generalize; say you want exactly the two privilege codes already specified and no others unless the agent says one of those codes is not available. If one is unavailable, proceed with the remaining available specified code.

4. For the required competency review appointment, state your preferred date is 2026-06-23 and your preferred time is 10:00 AM. If 10:00 AM on 2026-06-23 is available, accept it. If 10:00 AM is not available but other slots are offered on 2026-06-23, accept the earliest offered slot on 2026-06-23. If no slots are available on 2026-06-23, accept the earliest offered slot on the earliest alternative date the agent presents.
5. After the agent reads back the privilege reactivation details, confirm them exactly if they match. Then, when asked about EHR access restoration, request reactivate_full.

6. For the DEA transfer intent, provide your DEA number, the new facility code, the new state code, the transfer reason, and the effective date. Use new facility code RBTZ-21X, new state code GA, transfer reason facility_relocation, and effective date 2026-07-01.

10. Throughout the call, whenever the agent gives a summary before submitting something, respond with a clear confirmation if the summary is correct. If the summary contains an error, correct only the incorrect field and then confirm.

11. If the agent asks an unexpected but relevant question not otherwise specified here, answer using the exact values in information_required when possible. If the question asks you to choose among multiple valid options and no rule is already provided, choose the first option stated by the agent.

12. Before ending the call, make sure the privilege reactivation and DEA transfer have each been completed with a case ID, and that the malpractice update outcome has been clearly explained as not possible due to no record on file with direction to credentialing.
