user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You are relocating to a new facility in Texas for a role change, and you need HR to complete the credentialing transition by transferring your DEA registration, updating your malpractice coverage to your new policy, and submitting a provisional license extension while your new-state renewal is still pending.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a few credentialing updates I need help with for an upcoming relocation.

must_have_criteria: Your DEA transfer to the new facility and state is completed and you receive a case ID or confirmation number for the transfer.
Your malpractice coverage is updated to the new carrier and policy details, and you receive a case ID or confirmation number for the update.
Your provisional license extension request for 60 days is submitted successfully and you receive a case ID.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Complete provider verification first by giving your NPI, home facility code, and 4-digit PIN when asked. If asked to confirm identity details already provided, repeat them exactly from information_required.

2. Because DEA transfer requires higher security, complete the OTP step after provider verification. If asked to confirm the phone number on file, confirm it only if the agent states a number you recognize; if the agent says the number is not recognizable or cannot proceed, accept being told to visit HR in person and end the call. If asked for the OTP, provide the 6-digit code exactly from information_required.

3. After authentication is complete, state that you need three things handled in this order: DEA transfer first, malpractice update second, and license extension third. Do not interleave details across intents unless the agent specifically asks.

4. For the DEA transfer intent, provide your DEA number, new facility code, new state code, transfer reason, and effective date when asked. If the agent asks for the new state as a full name or abbreviation, use the abbreviation from information_required. If the agent reads back the DEA number, facility code, state code, or effective date, confirm only if they match exactly; otherwise correct the mismatch immediately.

5. If the agent asks you to confirm the DEA transfer before submission, say yes only after they read back the DEA number, new facility code, new state code, transfer reason, and effective date correctly. Stay on the call until you receive a transfer case ID or confirmation number.

6. For the malpractice update intent, provide the new carrier, new policy number, per-occurrence limit, aggregate limit, effective date, and expiration date when asked. If the agent asks whether this is a new insurer or a renewal, say it is a new policy with carrier coverys for coverage in the new state.

7. If the agent reads back malpractice details, confirm only if the carrier, policy number, per-occurrence limit, aggregate limit, effective date, and expiration date all match exactly. Then explicitly approve submission. Stay on the call until you receive a malpractice update case ID or confirmation number.

8. For the license extension intent, provide the state license number, extension type, and extension length when asked. If the agent asks you to choose among duration options, choose 60 days. If the agent asks whether the extension is provisional or supervised, choose provisional. Do not provide any supervising physician NPI because provisional extensions do not use one.

9. If the agent reads back the license extension request, confirm only if the license number, provisional extension type, and 60-day duration are all correct. Stay on the call until you receive a license extension case ID.

10. If the agent asks which request to handle first or offers a different order, insist on this deterministic order: DEA transfer first, then malpractice update, then license extension. If one completed intent is finished, move to the next unfinished intent in that same order.

11. For any unexpected but relevant question not covered here, answer only with facts from information_required. If the question asks for information not in information_required and not required by the scenario, say you do not have any additional details beyond what you already provided.

12. At the end, ask the agent to read back the outcome of all three requests and their case IDs or confirmation numbers. Once all three outcomes have been completed and read back, thank them and end the call.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a case ID or confirmation number for the DEA transfer, a case ID or confirmation number for the malpractice coverage update, and a case ID for the license extension request. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If asked whether the DEA transfer reason is facility_relocation, role_change, or additional_practice_site, choose role_change.
If asked whether the license extension is provisional or supervised, choose provisional.
If asked for a supervising physician NPI for the license extension, say there is no supervising physician because this is a provisional extension.
If asked to choose among extension lengths, choose 60 days.
If asked whether the malpractice coverage meets the minimum threshold, say the per-occurrence limit is one million dollars.

negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by completing provider verification with your NPI, current facility code, and PIN, then say you need help with three items: a DEA transfer, a malpractice update, and a license extension.

2. If the agent initiates OTP for the DEA transfer, confirm the phone number on file only if you recognize it, then provide the OTP code when asked. If the number is not one you recognize, accept being told this cannot be changed by phone and end the call.

3. For the DEA transfer, provide your DEA number, new facility code, new state code, and transfer reason. If the agent reads back any identifier or code, confirm it only if it exactly matches your information_required values; otherwise correct only the mismatched value.

4. If the agent asks for the transfer effective date, state 2026-07-01. If the agent cannot use that date but offers alternative future dates, accept the earliest offered date on or after 2026-07-01. If no offered date is on or after 2026-07-01, accept the first future date the agent says is valid.

8. Then handle the license extension as a separate item. Provide the state license number, say you want a provisional extension, and request 90 days.

9. If the agent asks you to choose among extension durations, choose 90 days. If the agent asks whether the extension is provisional or supervised, choose provisional. Do not volunteer or provide any supervising physician information because provisional extensions do not use one.

10. If the agent reads back the license information, confirm it only if the license number, extension type, and duration exactly match your information_required values; otherwise correct only the incorrect field. Stay on the call until you receive the license extension case ID and the agent confirms the required notification has been sent.

11. If the agent asks to process the three requests in a different order, accept any order as long as all three are completed in the same call. Handle one intent fully before moving to the next; do not interleave details unless the agent requires it.

12. If the agent asks unexpected but relevant follow-up questions that are necessary to complete one of these three requests, answer using only the values in information_required. If the question asks for information not provided there and not logically required by the scenario, say you do not have any additional information beyond what you already provided.
13. At the end, ask for a recap of all completed actions and their case IDs or confirmation numbers. Once all three outcomes are confirmed, end the call.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You recently had a baby and need to take care of three HR items in one call: add your newborn child to your visa petition, file FMLA bonding leave for the first weeks at home, and use a few PTO days right before that leave starts.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a few HR things I need help with today.

must_have_criteria: Your newborn child is added to your visa petition and you receive the amendment ID or other concrete confirmation that the dependent addition was completed.
Your FMLA bonding leave case is opened for the requested dates and you receive the FMLA case ID, with a return-to-work check-in actually scheduled.
Your PTO request for the requested dates is submitted and approved in the system, and you receive the PTO case ID with the deducted days confirmed.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Begin by completing authentication for the highest-required level of this call: provide your employee ID and date of birth when asked, then complete OTP verification. If the agent confirms the phone number by last four digits, simply confirm it is the correct number if it matches what they state; then provide the 6-digit OTP code when asked.

2. After authentication, say you need help with three things in this order: adding your newborn as a visa dependent, filing FMLA bonding leave, and requesting PTO for a few days before the leave starts.

3. First intent — visa dependent addition: provide the visa petition number, then provide the dependent's first and last name with spelling if asked, the relationship as child, the dependent's date of birth, the dependent's country of birth, and the USCIS receipt number. If the agent reads back either the visa petition number or USCIS receipt number, confirm only if it exactly matches the values in information_required; otherwise correct it.
4. If the agent asks any ambiguous relationship question for the visa request, answer that the dependent is your child. If they ask whether this is tied to a recent birth, say yes.

5. Second intent — FMLA: state that the leave category is bonding and provide the leave start date and leave end date. If the agent references eligibility, acknowledge and continue. If the agent asks for a return-to-work check-in preference, state your preferred date is 2026-09-21 and your preferred time is 10:00 AM.

6. For the FMLA return-to-work appointment, use this deterministic selection rule: if 2026-09-21 at 10:00 AM is available, accept it. If that exact time is not available but another slot is available on 2026-09-21, accept the earliest offered slot on 2026-09-21. If no slots are available on 2026-09-21, accept the earliest offered slot on the nearest future date that the agent offers, as long as it is on or after 2026-09-18.

7. Third intent — PTO: request PTO, not sick leave, for 2026-08-17 through 2026-08-19. If the agent explains how many days will be deducted, accept the calculation if they say those dates are eligible and confirm the request.

8. If the agent asks whether you want to change the PTO dates, use this deterministic fallback rule: first prefer the exact range 2026-08-17 through 2026-08-19. If any date in that range is unavailable, accept the earliest alternative consecutive 3-day range the agent offers that ends before 2026-08-24. If no 3-day range is offered, accept the earliest alternative 2-day range that ends before 2026-08-24.

9. Whenever the agent summarizes any submission before finalizing it, listen for read-backs of names, dates, and IDs. Confirm them only if they exactly match the values in information_required and the dates above; otherwise provide the corrected value directly.

10. If the agent asks unexpected but relevant follow-up questions not covered elsewhere, answer briefly using only the information in information_required and the scenario. Do not invent extra details.
11. Stay on the call until all three actions are completed with concrete confirmation numbers or IDs and the return-to-work check-in is actually scheduled.
12. After all confirmations are received, thank the agent briefly and end the call.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received concrete confirmation that your newborn child was added to your visa petition, an FMLA case ID for bonding leave with a scheduled return-to-work check-in, and a PTO case ID with the approved deducted days confirmed. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, or problems beyond what is defined here.

negotiation_behavior: 1. Complete authentication first. Provide your employee ID and date of birth when asked. user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a few HR things I need help with after having a baby.

must_have_criteria: Your newborn dependent is added to your visa petition amendment and you receive the amendment or confirmation ID.

nice_to_have_criteria: You want to file FMLA bonding leave for the planned leave period, but if you are told you are not eligible because you have not worked the required 1,250 hours in the past year, ask one follow-up question about the reason and then accept that outcome.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by saying you have a few HR items related to a recent birth. Complete authentication first: provide your employee ID and date of birth when asked, then complete OTP when asked for the elevated-auth portions of the call.

2. First intent - visa dependent addition: when the agent is ready for immigration details, provide your visa petition number. If asked, confirm it exactly as provided. Provide the dependent's first and last name and spell both names letter by letter. State that the relationship is child. Provide the dependent's date of birth and country of birth, then provide the USCIS receipt number for the petition amendment. If the agent reads back any of these identifiers or names, confirm them if correct; if any character is wrong, correct only the wrong character(s). Explicitly confirm when the agent asks permission to submit.

3. For the visa dependent addition, stay on the call until the agent confirms the change is completed and gives you the amendment or confirmation ID. A statement that they will process it later is not enough.

4. Second intent - FMLA: after the visa item is completed or clearly in progress to completion, say you also want to file bonding leave for time with your newborn. If asked for the leave category, say bonding. Provide the intended leave start date of 2026-09-21 and leave end date of 2026-10-16. If the agent checks eligibility and says you are not eligible because you have not worked 1,250 hours in the past year, ask exactly one follow-up question: 'Can you tell me how short I am on the hours requirement?' After they explain, accept the denial and do not argue further. Do not ask for exceptions, backdating, or alternative leave types unless the agent volunteers them.

5. Third intent - PTO: after the FMLA outcome is clear, ask to use sick leave for 2026-09-14 through 2026-09-16, the week before the planned leave period. If the agent asks whether to use general PTO or sick leave, choose sick leave. If the agent tells you the exact number of deducted days and which dates count, listen and explicitly confirm if those dates still match 2026-09-14 through 2026-09-16. If eligibility is confirmed, explicitly approve submission.

6. For the PTO request, if the agent says one or more of the requested dates are ineligible because of blackout periods or overlap and offers alternative date options, choose deterministically as follows: first, accept the earliest offered date range that starts on or after 2026-09-14 and is no longer than 3 consecutive calendar days; if multiple offered ranges meet that rule, choose the first one the agent presents; if no offered range starts on or after 2026-09-14 and is 3 days or fewer, decline alternatives and ask to keep the original request if possible, otherwise accept that the PTO cannot be completed.

7. Throughout the call, whenever the agent reads back names, IDs, receipt numbers, or dates, confirm them if correct and correct them immediately if incorrect. Do not provide extra narrative or new requests.

8. If the agent asks unexpected but relevant clarifying questions that are necessary to complete one of the three defined requests, answer using only the values in information_required. If the question asks for information not present there and not implied by the scenario, say you do not have any additional information beyond what you already provided.
9. After all three intents have been addressed, briefly confirm the outcomes: visa dependent added, FMLA denied for ineligibility after explanation, and PTO submitted if completed.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a confirmation or amendment ID for the visa dependent addition, received a PTO case ID with the approved deducted days confirmed for the PTO request, and received a clear explanation that FMLA bonding leave cannot be filed because you are not eligible based on hours worked. decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, says your request cannot be processed and there are no alternatives, says you must visit HR in person, or says they are encountering errors and cannot help you right now, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You were recently diagnosed with a condition that requires surgery. You need to file FMLA leave for the surgery and recovery period, swap an upcoming shift that conflicts with your pre-op appointments, and request a few sick days before the procedure so you can rest.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a few HR things I need help with today.

must_have_criteria: Your FMLA leave case is opened for your surgery and recovery period, and you receive the FMLA case ID and the scheduled return-to-work check-in details.
Your shift swap is confirmed for the specified upcoming shift with your certified colleague, and you receive the shift swap case ID.
Your sick leave request is submitted for the requested pre-surgery dates, the deducted days are confirmed to you, and you receive the PTO case ID.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Complete authentication first. Provide your employee ID and date of birth when asked. Because FMLA is part of the call, also complete OTP verification when prompted: confirm the phone number if it matches a number you recognize, then provide the 6-digit OTP code exactly as listed in information_required.

2. After authentication, give a high-level overview of all three needs in one sentence: you need to file FMLA for surgery recovery, swap one upcoming shift because of pre-op appointments, and request a few sick days before the leave starts.

3. First intent — FMLA: when asked for details, say the leave is for your own medical condition and provide the leave start date and leave end date exactly as listed in information_required. If asked for the leave category, choose employee_medical_condition.

4. For the FMLA return-to-work check-in, state your preferred date and preferred time exactly as listed in information_required. If that exact date and time are available, accept them. If the preferred time on that same date is unavailable, accept the earliest offered slot on that same date. If no slots are available on that date, accept the earliest offered slot on the nearest future date that the agent offers that is on or after the leave end date.

5. Before the FMLA is submitted, if the agent reads back the dates or category, listen for accuracy and confirm only if they exactly match the values in information_required. If any item is wrong, correct only the incorrect item and keep all other details unchanged.

6. Second intent — shift swap: after the FMLA portion is handled, say you also need to swap one upcoming shift because of pre-op appointments. Provide the shift ID and your colleague's employee ID exactly as listed in information_required. If asked for the unit, provide the unit code exactly as listed in information_required.

7. For the shift swap, if the agent reads back the shift ID, colleague employee ID, or unit code, confirm only if each matches information_required exactly. If one item is wrong, correct that item only. If the agent offers multiple ways to proceed, accept the first option that results in confirming the swap for that same shift with that same colleague.

8. Third intent — PTO: after the shift swap is handled, say you also want to request sick leave for the few days before surgery so you can rest. Provide the PTO type and the sick leave start and end dates exactly as listed in information_required. If asked whether this is general PTO or sick leave, choose sick leave.

9. For the PTO request, when the agent tells you how many days will be deducted and which dates count, confirm if the request still covers the same date range you provided. Do not change the requested date range. If the agent reads back the dates or PTO type incorrectly, correct only the incorrect item.

10. At the end, stay on the call until you have concrete confirmation for all three completed actions: an FMLA case ID plus return-to-work check-in details, a shift swap case ID, and a PTO case ID with deducted days confirmed.

11. If the agent asks an unexpected question that is not covered by the information here, give only the exact value from information_required if one exists. If no such value exists, say you do not have additional details and restate the request without inventing new information.

End the call.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You were recently diagnosed with a condition that requires surgery and recovery time. You need to file FMLA for the treatment period, swap a shift you cannot work because of a pre-op appointment, and ask for a few sick days before the FMLA starts to rest, even though that sick-leave request will be denied due to insufficient balance.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a few HR things I need help with today.

must_have_criteria: Your FMLA leave is filed successfully for your medical condition, and you receive the FMLA case ID.
Your shift swap is confirmed with your colleague for the specified shift, and you receive the shift swap case ID.
nice_to_have_criteria: You want the sick leave request approved for the requested pre-surgery dates, but if you are told it is not possible because of insufficient PTO balance, ask one follow-up question about your remaining balance and then accept that outcome.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by completing authentication. Provide your employee ID and date of birth when asked. Because FMLA is part of the call, complete OTP verification too: confirm the last four digits of the phone number on file if they match what the agent says, then provide the OTP code.

2. After authentication, briefly say you need help with three things: filing FMLA for an upcoming surgery, swapping one shift because of a pre-op appointment, and requesting a few sick days before the leave starts.

3. First intent — FMLA: when asked for details, say the leave is for your own medical condition and provide leave category employee_medical_condition, leave start date 2026-08-10, and leave end date 2026-09-21.

4. If the agent needs a return-to-work check-in preference for the FMLA case, state your preferred date is 2026-09-22 and your preferred time is 10:00 AM. If 10:00 AM on 2026-09-22 is available, accept it. If not, accept the earliest offered slot on 2026-09-22. If no slots are available on 2026-09-22, accept the earliest offered slot on the nearest future date.
5. When the agent reads back the FMLA details, confirm them exactly if they match your information. Do not approve submission until the dates and leave category are correct.

6. Second intent — shift swap: after the FMLA is handled, ask to swap your shift with your colleague because of a pre-op appointment conflict. Provide shift_id SHF-20260806-2300 and colleague employee ID EMP769989.

7. If the agent reads back the shift ID or colleague employee ID, confirm only if they match exactly. If they differ, correct them. Accept the swap as soon as the agent confirms it is eligible and completed.
8. Third intent — PTO: after the shift swap is handled, request sick leave from 2026-08-03 through 2026-08-05 to rest before surgery. If asked whether this is PTO or sick leave, choose sick leave.

9. If the agent says the sick leave request cannot be approved because of insufficient balance, ask one follow-up question: ask what your current sick leave balance is. After the agent answers, accept the denial and do not propose alternate dates.

10. At the end, stay on the call until you have the FMLA case ID, the shift swap case ID, the return-to-work check-in details, and a clear statement of the denied sick leave request with your current balance.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a case ID for the FMLA filing, your return-to-work check-in has been scheduled, you have received a case ID for the shift swap, and you have been clearly told that the sick leave request was denied due to insufficient balance along with your current sick leave balance. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You are a new hospital hire in your first week and want to finish all of your basic HR setup in one call: complete your pending onboarding tasks, submit your I-9 work authorization document, and request two PTO days for a family event you had planned before you started.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a few HR things I need to take care of today.

must_have_criteria: Your pending onboarding tasks are marked complete and your orientation follow-up appointment is scheduled with a confirmed date and time.
Your I-9 verification is submitted successfully and you receive a case ID.
Your PTO request is submitted successfully for the requested date range, the exact deducted days are confirmed, and you receive a case ID.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by giving your employee ID and date of birth for authentication, then say you need help with onboarding, I-9, and a PTO request in that order.

2. For the onboarding intent, say you need to complete two pending tasks: OCC_HEALTH and HIPAA_TRAIN. Provide the completion code for each task only when asked, in this order: first OCC_HEALTH, then HIPAA_TRAIN.

3. When the orientation follow-up is being scheduled, first request 2026-08-17 at 10:00 AM. If 10:00 AM on 2026-08-17 is available, accept it. If 10:00 AM is not available but another slot is available on 2026-08-17, accept the earliest offered slot on 2026-08-17. If no slots are available on 2026-08-17, accept the earliest offered slot on the nearest future alternative date.

4. After onboarding is completed, move to the I-9 intent. State that this is an initial verification and that you are using a List A document. Provide the document type, number, expiration date, and issuing country only when asked. If the agent reads back the document number, listen carefully and confirm only if it matches exactly.

5. After the I-9 is submitted, move to the PTO intent. Request PTO, not sick leave, for 2026-08-20 through 2026-08-21 for a family event. Wait for the agent to tell you the exact number of PTO days that will be deducted and which dates count, then explicitly confirm if they match the requested range.
6. If the agent asks you to confirm a summary before any submission, say yes only if all read-back details exactly match the information in information_required.

7. If the agent asks an unexpected but relevant clarifying question, answer briefly using only the data in information_required. If the question asks for information not provided there, say you do not have any additional details beyond what you already shared.
8. Once all three intents are completed and you have the confirmation details, thank the agent, answer no if asked whether you need anything else, and end the call.

9. For any yes/no confirmation before submission, say yes only when the summary exactly matches the information_required values for that intent. If any value is wrong, correct only the wrong value and continue.

10. If the agent asks an unexpected but relevant clarification question, answer only with information already present in information_required. Do not invent additional facts, reasons, blackout dates, or alternate colleagues.
11. After all three items are completed and the confirmation numbers have been read back, briefly confirm that everything sounds right and wrap up the call.

You speak curtly, getting straight to what you need without much small talk or pleasantries. You want the system to be fast and efficient, and you'll show your frustration if things move slowly or require extra steps.

scenario_context: Triple intent: Payroll Correction + On-Call Registration + Shift Swap (Flows 7+9+2). Nurse is doing end-of-week schedule housekeeping. They noticed their on-call hours from a recent shift were not recorded correctly on their timesheet, they want to register for on-call availability for the next two weeks since the unit needs coverage, and they need to swap an upcoming shift with a colleague due to a scheduling conflict. All three intents are fully satisfiable. Different caller from T7.1 — this nurse is correcting overtime hours rather than on-call hours, and swapping a different shift.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You are doing end-of-week schedule housekeeping and need to fix a payroll issue from a recent shift, register yourself for primary on-call coverage for the next two weeks, and also ask about swapping an upcoming shift with a coworker even though that swap may not be allowed.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a few HR scheduling and payroll things I need help with today.

must_have_criteria: Your payroll correction for the specified shift is submitted successfully and you receive a case ID.
Your on-call availability for the specified two-week window on your unit is registered successfully and you receive a registration ID.
nice_to_have_criteria: You want to swap your upcoming shift with your colleague, but if you are told the swap cannot be processed because the shift is within the lockout window, ask one follow-up question and then accept that outcome.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by completing authentication first. If asked, give your employee ID and date of birth. After authentication, say you have three things to handle: a payroll correction, on-call registration, and a shift swap.

2. For the payroll correction intent, provide the payroll correction shift ID, correction type, and corrected total hours only when asked. If the agent reads back the shift ID or hours, confirm them exactly if they match the information_required fields.
3. If the agent confirms the payroll correction is eligible, explicitly approve submission. Stay on the call until you receive the payroll correction case ID.

4. For the on-call registration intent, provide the unit code first if asked, then the availability start date and end date, then say you want the primary tier. If asked about blackout dates, say there are none and use an empty list.

5. If the agent offers to review existing on-call registrations or asks you to confirm the window, confirm the exact dates from information_required. If registration is allowed, explicitly approve submission. Stay on the call until you receive the on-call registration ID.

6. For the shift swap intent, provide the upcoming shift ID and your colleague's employee ID when asked. If the agent says the shift cannot be swapped because it is within the 24-hour lockout window, ask exactly one follow-up question: 'So there's no way to process that swap this close to the shift?'
7. After the agent answers that follow-up, accept the denial without arguing further and move on. Do not propose a workaround, different colleague, or different shift.

8. At the end, if the agent summarizes all outcomes, confirm only if the summary matches: payroll correction submitted with case ID, on-call registration completed with registration ID, and shift swap denied due to the lockout window.

9. If the agent asks unexpected but relevant clarifying questions, answer only with values from information_required. If the question asks for a preference not defined there, give the deterministic answer already specified here: no blackout dates, primary on-call tier, and no additional requests.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a case ID for the payroll correction, a registration ID for the on-call availability registration, and a clear explanation that the shift swap cannot be processed because the shift is within the 24-hour lockout window. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If asked whether there are any blackout dates within the on-call availability window, say no and provide an empty list.
If asked whether the on-call registration should be primary or backup, choose primary.
If asked to confirm which shift is for payroll correction versus which shift is for the attempted swap, use the payroll_shift_id for the payroll correction and the shift_id for the swap.

You speak curtly, getting straight to what you need without much small talk or pleasantries. You want the system to be fast and efficient, and you'll show your frustration if things move slowly or require extra steps.

scenario_context: Triple intent: Payroll Correction + On-Call Registration + Shift Swap (Flows 7+9+2). Nurse is doing end-of-week schedule housekeeping. They noticed their on-call hours from a recent shift were not recorded correctly on their timesheet, they want to register for on-call availability for the next two weeks since the unit needs coverage, and they need to swap an upcoming shift with a colleague due to a scheduling conflict. The payroll correction (Flow 7) and on-call registration (Flow 9) are satisfiable, but the shift swap (Flow 2) fails because the upcoming shift is within the 24-hour lockout window (check_swap_eligibility returns 'swap_locked'). Agent should complete the payroll correction and on-call registration, and inform the caller the swap cannot proceed.
